diagnostic_finding: Predictions show generic texture-correlated activation spread across entire image, uncorrelated with ground-truth tamper location, consistent across all visually inspected samples. Most likely explanation: (1) cross-dataset generalization gap — Mantra-Net trained on its own synthetic dataset, not CASIA/DEFACTO; (2) possible noise-domain signal degradation from JPEG re-compression (quality 90) applied during our dataset preparation, compounding any pre-existing compression from CASIA's source images. This is consistent with known cross-dataset generalization weaknesses documented in forensics literature and reinforces this thesis's stated problem motivation.
engineering_note: Mantra-Net's original TF1.15/Keras code was successfully bridged via an isolated legacy conda environment (Python 3.7, TF1.15, Keras 2.2.4, protobuf 3.20.3) after resolving three sequential framework incompatibilities: removed legacy Keras APIs, protobuf version conflict, and inherited eager-execution behavior. CPU-only inference due to L4 GPU architecture predating TF1.15's compiled CUDA kernels.
